title: Vibrant African sounds with 'Songlines'
bodyText:
Gallery

GEORGE ENTERTAINMENT NEWS - This weekend, the Market Community Theatre will resonate with deep, moving African sounds during two performances of Songlines South Africa.

Following a lively opening night on Friday 18 July, theatre-goers can expect an unforgettable experience.

Last week's opening saw attendees gathered around a fire outside the venue, where a youth choir set a captivating tone for the evening.


> PHOTO GALLERY: Songlines South Africa at the Market Community Theatre


The choir, part of Siyakhona Youth Development - an NPO in Thembalethu led by Xolisa, Siyabonga and Simbongile Mtishelwa - delighted the audience with their performances of traditional dance, drama and song.

Theatre director Heather Stead shared that the show came together in just three days. She described the process as both nerve-racking and creatively dynamic.

Beyond its cultural significance, Songlines South Africa serves as a fundraiser for the Market Theatre, supporting its ongoing community efforts.

Sinesipho Buwu and Inga Nellani. Photo: Michelle Pienaar

Cathari Johnson, Willow Fourie, Tasia du Toit (chair of the George Society of Arts) and Xolisa Mtishelwa.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
